provenance.source_corpus: The Restoration of Elk ( Cervus elaphus ) in Ontario, Canada: 1998–2005

source_spans.text: Wolf predation has continued to be an important factor in some winters in the NFR and also the LOW ERAs of Ontario.
Wolf predation and illegal shooting are limiting elk populations in two of the northern Ontario release areas.
Wolf predation and illegal shooting are limiting elk populations in two of the northern Ontario release areas.
In this study, elk were held for considerable periods of time prior to release in Ontario (i.e., soft release as opposed to a hard release).
Those elk tended to remain closer to the release area and mortality has been lower than during years when elk were hard released or only held for a short period of time.
Recent mild winters have contributed to the increased survival of elk in Ontario.
Recent mild winters have contributed to the increased survival of elk in Ontario.
The Ontario elk restoration program has met with great success primarily due to the collaborative, multipartnered approach to wildlife management.
The Ontario elk restoration program has met with great success primarily due to the collaborative, multipartnered approach to wildlife management.
stress-related emaciation (13%) (partially due to the stress of relocation)
stress-related emaciation (13%) (partially due to the stress of relocation)
elk were extirpated by the late 1800s as a result of several factors including unregulated hunting, market hunting, and the eradication of elk on lands targeted for human settlement and agriculture.
elk were extirpated by the late 1800s as a result of several factors including unregulated hunting, market hunting, and the eradication of elk on lands targeted for human settlement and agriculture.
Further shipments have not occurred due to the threat of chronic wasting disease (CWD).
Further shipments have not occurred due to the threat of chronic wasting disease (CWD).

nodes.name: Length of time animals were kept in pens prior to release

source_spans.text: In some areas, dispersion of elk appeared to be related to the length of time animals were kept in pens prior to release.
In some areas, dispersion of elk appeared to be related to the length of time animals were kept in pens prior to release.
